lesson: Use __init_subclass__(cls, *, name=None) with a class-level dict to auto-register subclasses without a decorator.
Give a reusable descriptor __set_name__(self, owner, name) so it derives its private storage key from the attribute name it's bound to.
Implement __getattr__ (not __getattribute__) for lazy/cached fallback lookups since it only fires on attribute-lookup misses, and cache in it to avoid repeated loader calls.
Use inspect.signature(func).bind(*a,**kw) plus apply_defaults() to validate arguments against parameter annotations before dispatching a dynamic call.
Store a data descriptor's per-instance value under a name-mangled key in obj.__dict__ (never the plain attribute name) so __get__/__set__ don't recurse and instance __dict__ writes can't shadow it.
Enforce interface contracts in type.__new__ by checking inspect.signature(namespace[method]).parameters against a required param list before the subclass is even created.
Override metaclass __prepare__ to return a dict subclass whose __setitem__ rejects re-assigning the same class-body name, catching silent method-shadowing bugs at class-creation time.

Back a descriptor's per-instance state with weakref.WeakKeyDictionary (never a plain dict) when the owner uses __slots__, so instances without a __dict__ still get storage that doesn't leak on deletion.
